description: Known global/static memory addresses with effects and notes. Useful for RAM watches, patch points, and locating data-backed behavior.
Known function entry points with descriptions and register notes. Useful for debugger lookups and call-site interpretation.
Move table and action-state structure offsets. Useful when mapping fighter state constants and animation/action metadata.
Mixed ID tables for characters, stages, CPU AI, items, elements, status flags, SFX, name tags, and music.
Static stage info structure offsets. Useful for camera bounds, spawn tables, collision metadata, and stage-specific constants.
GObj/entity structure layout notes. Useful for object ownership, links, callbacks, and entity lifecycle fields.
Hitbox structure offsets and field semantics. Useful for subaction event decoding and fighter/item collision behavior.
Game-over object/view offsets with default hex/value/effect data.
Hurtbox structure offset notes. Useful for collision body layout and fighter data interpretation.
Player/common constant offsets and defaults. Useful for controller, movement, and shared gameplay tuning values.
Bone lookup table and body-part mapping notes. Useful for model joint lookups, hitbox attachment, and animation data.
StartMelee match setup structure layout. Useful for VS setup, special mode setup, and match initialization data.
Static and dynamic player/fighter data offsets. Useful for field naming, struct reconstruction, and debugger memory inspection.
Per-character attribute table. Columns are attribute offsets; rows are characters. Useful for constants, float data, and character tuning.
Subaction event binary layouts, including hitbox event bitfields. Useful for action script decoding and assembly-to-data interpretation.
Character select screen offsets and default values.
Debug menu memory map plus character and stage ID mappings.
Free or unused memory ranges with notes. Useful for patch experiments and understanding gaps in the address space.
